hypothesis: edge_naive (no lag/fee handling) is a control, not a strategy — its backtest edge should collapse out-of-sample.
note: Backtest +13.76/g → forward +1.29/g. Confirms lag/fee fragility. Keep as baseline-to-beat only; never deploy.

hypothesis: Holding a cheap favorite to settlement harvests Kalshi's fee curve (fee max at 50c, ~0 at extremes; settlement is free) — a structural edge.
note: auto_house (Q4-only variant) backtest north-star 0.625 on 246 games. Forward n=18 bets still tiny. EXP-004 confirms the Q4 regime is the cleanest expression of this structural edge. Watch the forward win rate (gate: >55% OOS to confirm calibration).

hypothesis: Small confirmed edges (~3-8c) on favorites beat large model-vs-market divergences, which are mostly stale-model error.
note: FALSIFIED on 437 backtest trades. 15c+ bucket had the HIGHEST per-trade P&L (+$3.94/t, same 49% win rate as 8-15c range). Large gaps are not stale-model noise — they often reflect genuinely cheap Q4 favorites. The confound: large gaps also correlate with cheap entry prices, buying more contracts per dollar at stake. Killed; mechanism was wrong.

hypothesis: Late-game (Q4) entries are far more reliable than early-game, where win-prob is noisy.
note: VALIDATED on 437 trades across 246 games. Q4+: 69% win rate, +$4.88/trade. Q1-Q3: 39% win rate, +$1.89/trade. ESPN model at 70-75% in Q1-Q3 has only 29% actual win rate — severe miscalibration. In Q4 the model is accurate (overall 69% rate). Worst games were dominated by early entries. This directly motivated auto_house EXP-004.

hypothesis: Restricting auto_house entries to the final period (Q4+) eliminates the miscalibrated early-game region and improves north-star robustness.
note: INVALIDATED 2026-05-27 by the C1 look-ahead fix. The 0.625 north-star was a LOOK-AHEAD LEAK: engine.simulate filled at a 1-min price bar that ended up to ~60s BEFORE the play whose win-prob it traded on, booking the market's own catch-up as 'edge'. After filling at the bar that closes AFTER the signal (honest), auto_house is -0.56/game (was +2.82); edge_naive control -10.45 (was +11.82). The Q4 'edge' (EXP-003) was the same artifact. No deployable edge exists; matches EDGE_SCAN (EXP-005).

hypothesis: The ESPN-WP vs Kalshi-mid gap predicts settlement -> trading the gap (hold to settlement) is +EV in some regime.
note: KILLED on 246 games / 118k plays (research/edge_discovery.py). Kalshi mid and ESPN WP near-identically calibrated (Brier 0.1181 vs 0.1181); ESPN WP carries ~no info the market hasn't priced. Mispricing scan: every apparent survivor is in clutch time and, once clustered by GAME, filled at the ask/bid actually crossed, fresh-price-guarded, and judged on the TAIL, has a 46-55% LOSING-game rate and CVaR(20%) of -33 to -51c; positive mean is outlier-driven (median bet loses). No tradeable predictive edge in any regime. The whole gap-trading family (model_revert/conviction/wp_momentum) sits on this dead premise.

hypothesis: Signed order-flow bursts predict the NEXT short-horizon price move (microstructure), independent of settlement.
note: KILLED 2026-05-27 (research/edge_discovery.py --flow, docs/FLOW_SCAN.md, 291 games). Signed order-flow does NOT predict the next mid move: GROSS forward return is noise centered on zero across all horizons (30/60/120s) and flow strengths (50 to 1k+ contracts), -0.27 to +0.14c, ~50% directional; only 2 cells survive OOS and at ~0.0c. NET after round-trip (2x spread + 2 fees) uniformly -2.3 to -3.7c. The market incorporates flow ~instantly (efficient at this timescale). Combined with EXP-005 (gap has no predictive edge), BOTH predictive theses are dead -> the market is sharp on price AND flow. Remaining edge must be STRUCTURAL/execution (spread capture / market-making, favorite-longshot bias, cross-venue de-vig arb), not prediction.

hypothesis: auto_house's Q4 edge (north-star 0.625) is REAL and not the same outlier/tail illusion as EXP-005.
note: RESOLVED 2026-05-27: NOT real. auto_house's 0.625 was look-ahead leakage in engine.simulate (stale fill bar), not a structural fee/bail edge. Fixed via fill-at-next-bar; honest auto_house = -0.56/game, worst -29.5. North-star fell 0.625 -> -5.265 (best actual strategy run_momentum -1.22/g). The whole gap-trading roster is negative once priced honestly. See LAB_REVIEW C1.

hypothesis: Kalshi diverges from the sharp sportsbook (DraftKings) line enough to arb (cross-venue / closing-line value).
note: KILLED — now RIGOROUS (redone 2026-05-27 after the 34-game version was too fast). research/cross_venue.py on 294 games, one independent pre-game bet/game, expectancy-framed (small REAL +EV would show). Kalshi vs de-vig DK closing line: Brier 0.1928 vs 0.1929 (identical), divergence mean 1.2c / max 4.0c / 0 games >5c. Betting the divergence toward the book: exp -0.04c/bet (all 294), -8.76c (>=2c), +0.27c on just 12 bets (>=3c, noise); OOS sign-flips (-12c disc -> +1c hold). No small-but-real edge to scale. Kalshi is tightly coupled to the sharp line. Pre-game cross-venue arb conclusively dead.

hypothesis: Kalshi price LAGS the live win-prob signal intra-game, leaving a tradeable window.
note: NEGATIVE (first read, SMALL SAMPLE — only 2 captured games had enough tick data; 41 win-prob jumps). research/lead_lag.py: cross-corr(Δwin-prob, Δmid) ~0 at every lag -8..+8 (max |corr| 0.02 = noise); event study around >=3c win-prob jumps shows the mid moves only +-0.1c in the +-10s window. Price does NOT lag the signal — it barely reacts to win-prob jumps because it already reflects game state. No exploitable window, and our ESPN 5s poll is slower than Kalshi's feed anyway. Consistent with EXP-005/006/008. CAVEAT: n=2 games; the daemon keeps capturing, so re-run as more accumulate, but the direction is clear and matches everything. RE-RUN 2026-05-29: lead_lag.py re-run with 13 captured games; still only 2 games have win_prob in ticks (newer NBA captures missing game state — see OPEN_QUESTIONS.md). Status unchanged: killed-provisional. The missing win_prob is a capture infrastructure issue, not a new finding.

hypothesis: Passive market-making (spread capture) is +EV and scalable on Kalshi sports.
note: PARTIAL/FEE-GATED (research/market_making.py, 214,801 fills across 7 captured games). The MM edge is REAL gross: realized spread (half-spread minus adverse selection) = +0.35c/contract at 30s, +0.29c at 60s; adverse selection is mild (median spread 1.0c, mostly retained). BUT maker fee ~1.13c/contract (assuming makers pay the standard 0.07*p*(1-p) formula) => NET -0.78c/contract. So MM is fee-gated, not edge-gated: the gross edge exists and is scalable, but Kalshi's retail per-fill fee exceeds the ~1c spread. LEVER: a Kalshi maker-fee reduction/rebate (liquidity-provider program, a business/volume arrangement) would flip MM to ~+0.35c/contract scalable. VERIFY the actual maker fee Kalshi charges us (I assumed taker formula) — if makers already pay less, recompute. BREAKEVEN QUANTIFIED: +EV iff maker fee < ~31% of taker (<=0.35c/contract). At full maker exemption +0.35c, at 25%-of-taker +0.07c, at 50% -0.22c. Kalshi charges makers a REDUCED schedule (confirmed via help center: resting orders fee-exempt-ish/reduced to encourage MM), so likely in the +EV zone, but VERIFY the exact NBA/WNBA maker coefficient (fee PDF was 429 rate-limited; or read realized fee on real maker fills). SETTLED (no real trade needed — fee is public): Kalshi maker coef = 0.0175 vs taker 0.07 = 25% of taker, BELOW the 31% breakeven. So MM net = +0.07c/contract idealized — technically +EV but razor-thin, and real queue competition + worse live adverse selection likely erase it. Not a robust business. Verdict: a real-but-marginal edge, not worth building over better opportunities.

hypothesis: Kalshi NBA/WNBA in-game prices exhibit a Favorite-Longshot Bias (FLB) — favorites or longshots are systematically over/under-priced at specific probability ranges.
note: KILLED 2026-05-29 (research/flb_scan.py, 305 backtest games). Ran calibration scan at T-15/30/60/120min before settlement, split by league (248 NBA, 57 WNBA). No systematic FLB pattern in any direction. Weighted avg bias: -0.43c at T-15, +0.40c at T-30, -0.87c at T-60, -0.33c at T-120. Only 1 of 40 tested buckets reached significance (T-120min, 0.20-0.30 bucket: n=27, bias=+0.225, CI=[+0.05,+0.40]), consistent with the ~2 expected false positives from multiple comparison. Market is well-calibrated end-to-end. No actionable signal. Literature (FLB in sportsbooks = favorites underpriced) does NOT apply to Kalshi: the prediction market is tightly coupled to sharp sportsbook lines (EXP-008). Consistent with EXP-005/008.

hypothesis: Teams priced 20-30% on Kalshi early in game (T-120min before settlement, roughly Q1-Q2) win materially more than priced, because the market overreacts to early-game scoring runs.
note: PROMISING-PROVISIONAL (2026-05-29, research/flb_scan.py + mechanism check). T-120min 0.20-0.30 bucket: n=27, avg_p=0.257, win_rt=0.481, bias=+0.225, CI=[+0.05,+0.40]. MECHANISM CONFIRMED: all 27 games are early-deficit cases (0/27 pre-game underdogs). Avg T-180 price (60min earlier) was 0.535 (near-even-chance teams) that fell 27c to 25% in early game (approx Q1-Q2). TIME-SPLIT HOLDS: first half n=13 bias=+0.213, second half n=14 bias=+0.236 — consistent across regular season AND playoffs. League split: NBA n=18 win_rt=0.389 (+13c bias), WNBA n=9 win_rt=0.667 (+41c bias). Strategy: buy YES-team at 20-30% price when (a) game is early (T-90+ to settlement), (b) team was at 35%+ within last 60min (early-deficit, not pre-game dog). Hold to settlement. Expected edge: +22c/contract at 48% win rate. Kelly f* ~ 30%, use 1/4-Kelly (~7.5% stake). BLOCKERS: (1) live capture broken — need in-game game state to execute, (2) need 40+ OOS bets for validation (~9% trigger rate = ~450 more games = months). MULTIPLE-COMPARISON CAVEAT still applies (1 of 40 buckets), but time-split + mechanism check materially reduce the multiple-comparison concern. Gate: first 15 OOS bets must show win_rt > 35% to continue; retire if win_rt < 35% at n=40.
